message:
🐛 Fix stat block definitions to match actual handler IDs (#182)

* Fix stat block definitions to match actual handler IDs

8 dashboard stat block definition arrays had IDs that didn't match
what getDashboardStatValue or useUniversalStats actually handle,
causing "Not available on this dashboard" for all affected stats.

Fixed: Compliance (soc2→cis_score etc), DataCompliance (13 broken IDs),
Compute (cpu_usage→cpus etc), Events (info→normal, added recent),
Cost (compute_cost→cpu_cost etc), Alerts (total_alerts→firing etc),
Dashboard main (alerts→healthy etc), Operators (10 broken IDs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* Clean stale stat IDs from saved localStorage configs

When stat block definitions change IDs, users may have old stale IDs
saved in localStorage. These would persist and show "Not available".
Added a filter step in useStatBlockConfig to remove saved blocks
whose IDs no longer exist in ANY stat block definition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* Add missing tpus and memory_util to compute stat definitions

The compute dashboard handler supports these stat IDs but they
were omitted from the definitions during the ID alignment fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
